title: Module Check: Space Complexity and Recursion Depth

questions.question: What determines the call stack depth of a recursive DFS on a tree?
questions.answer: The height h of the tree — O(log n) for balanced, O(n) for skewed/degenerate

questions.question: Why is sys.setrecursionlimit(10**6) insufficient for very deep Python recursion?
questions.answer: The OS limits the C call stack (typically ~8MB, ~20K-40K Python frames); Python's limit doesn't override the OS limit

questions.question: BFS space = O(w) and DFS space = O(h). For a balanced binary tree, which is smaller?
questions.answer: DFS: O(log n) vs BFS: O(n/2). DFS is more space-efficient for balanced trees.

questions.question: What is the correct fix for Python RecursionError on a large DFS?
questions.answer: Convert to iterative DFS using an explicit `stack = []` (heap-allocated, no OS limit)
